title: Persist task-filter preferences

brief.desiredBehavior: Remember the active task filter across reloads, validating stored values before applying them.
acceptanceCriteria: The selected filter survives a reload.
Invalid stored values safely fall back to the default.

planQuestions: What state should survive a reload?
Where would you initialize that state?
What stored value should be treated as invalid?

hints.text: Think about when a preference is read versus when it is written.
What values should storage be allowed to restore?
Inspect the filter state initialization and selection method.

explainBackQuestion: Why should a stored preference be validated before it changes the UI?
